parameters.query:
INSERT INTO leads (domain, company_name, founder_name, founder_email, founder_linkedin_url, founder_ig_handle, source, source_metadata, icp_tier, is_archived, archive_reason)
VALUES ($1,$2,$3,$4,$5,$6,$7,$8::jsonb,$9::icp_tier_enum,$10,$11)
ON CONFLICT (LOWER(domain)) DO NOTHING
RETURNING id;

INSERT INTO outreach_events (lead_id, event_type, event_data, actor)
SELECT id, 'source', $2::jsonb, 'system' FROM leads WHERE id = $1;

parameters.content:
## Source · StoreLeads (manual CSV)
StoreLeads free tier is UI-only, so Julia exports a weekly CSV matching the ecom ICP (80K+ list & $70+ AOV, OR 60–100K & $27+ AOV) into a watched Drive folder. This ingests it, runs the disqualifier, tags `secondary_ecom`, and upserts.

**Set:** `Google Drive (FC)` OAuth credential; replace `REPLACE_FOLDER_ID` with the watched folder id; `Postgres · warm_outreach`.

**CSV columns** (best-effort mapped): domain, company_name, email, list_size, aov, platform. Missing headers are tolerated.

Full list/AOV gating is a Phase 4 concern — this only assigns the tier.
